description: Stable public error codes. Codes are APPEND-ONLY within contract 1.x: prose may improve, a code's meaning may not change, and a code is never reused. Tests assert on `code` and `instancePath`, never on prose.

policy.ordering: Errors are ordered deterministically: by stage in the order declared in `stages`, then by Unicode code-point order of `instancePath`, then by `code`, then by `validatorId`.
policy.boundedness: `actualSummary` is a bounded type/length category, never raw caller content. Diagnostics never echo an entire offending token, a secret, a filesystem path, or a large array.
policy.cap: At most 32 error records are returned. When more exist, one final `ERROR_LIMIT_REACHED` record is appended with the omitted count.
policy.repairsAreData: A `repair` is structured data — {operation, path, value?, reasonCode} — never free-form instruction-shaped prose. Repairs are never auto-applied by `validate`; `applySafeRepairs` accepts only allow-listed operations and revalidates from the start.
policy.blueprintNote: The blueprint names one code `LEGACY_AMBIGUOUS_CONNECTIVITY_MATRIX` while also requiring every code to sit in a declared namespace. The namespace rule is the structural one, so that code is registered here as `MIGRATION_AMBIGUOUS_CONNECTIVITY_MATRIX` with identical meaning.
policy.negativeZero: Negative zero is ACCEPTED, not rejected. RFC 8785 defines its canonical form as `0`, and -0 === 0, so nothing scientific is lost and digests stay reproducible. There is deliberately no error code for it: rejecting -0 would fail real exported data for a difference that canonicalization already erases. The obligation this creates is on the Python canonicalizer, whose `json.dumps(-0.0)` emits `-0.0` and MUST special-case it to `0` — a cross-language parity test asserts exactly this.

codes.summary: The input contained no JSON value.
codes.correctiveAction: Send a single complete JSON document.

codes.summary: The input is not well-formed JSON.
codes.correctiveAction: Fix the reported syntax position. Cortexel never accepts a fault-tolerant parse.

codes.summary: An object contained the same member name twice.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove the duplicate member. Which value would have won is undefined, so the document is rejected rather than silently resolved. This check is only possible on raw JSON text; it cannot be performed on an already-materialized JavaScript value.

codes.summary: Content followed the top-level JSON value.
codes.correctiveAction: Send exactly one top-level value.

codes.summary: A numeric token is not a valid JSON number.
codes.correctiveAction: Use JSON number grammar. NaN, Infinity, hex, and leading '+' are not JSON.

codes.summary: A number is outside the finite binary64 model.
codes.correctiveAction: Cortexel accepts only finite binary64 values. Represent a missing observation as null, not as a non-finite number.

codes.summary: The input contained a malformed or lone-surrogate escape sequence.
codes.correctiveAction: Emit well-formed UTF-8 with paired surrogates.

codes.summary: The input began with a byte-order mark.
codes.correctiveAction: Strip the BOM. The parser profile rejects it in both TypeScript and Python.

codes.summary: The input contained a comment.
codes.correctiveAction: Comments are not JSON. Remove them.

codes.summary: The input contained a trailing comma.
codes.correctiveAction: Trailing commas are not JSON. Remove them.

codes.summary: Nesting depth exceeded the parser limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Flatten the document. The limit is enforced during scanning, before any parse tree is allocated.

codes.summary: The raw input exceeded the byte limit for the active budget profile.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the payload, or reference bulk arrays with a DataRef instead of inlining them.

codes.summary: The input exceeded the total token limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the document size or use data references.

codes.summary: A single string exceeded the maximum string length.
codes.correctiveAction: Shorten the string. Labels and notes have explicit bounded lengths.

codes.summary: A numeric token was longer than any meaningful binary64 literal.
codes.correctiveAction: Emit an ordinary binary64 literal. A very long digit string cannot add precision.

codes.summary: An array exceeded the maximum member count.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the array or use a data reference.

codes.summary: An object exceeded the maximum member count.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the object's members.

codes.summary: An object used a prototype-polluting member name.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove members named __proto__, constructor, or prototype.

codes.summary: A property is defined by a getter or setter.
codes.correctiveAction: Pass plain data. Cortexel inspects property descriptors and will not invoke a caller accessor to discover a value.

codes.summary: An object carried a symbol-keyed property.
codes.correctiveAction: Pass a JSON-compatible object.

codes.summary: A value was a class instance, Map, Set, Date, Promise, Proxy, or other non-plain object.
codes.correctiveAction: Convert to a plain object or array before validating. Cortexel does not call toJSON, valueOf, or Symbol.toPrimitive on untrusted values.

codes.summary: An array had holes.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a dense array. Represent a missing observation as an explicit null.

codes.summary: An array carried named (non-index) own properties.
codes.correctiveAction: Pass a plain dense array with index properties only.

codes.summary: The value graph contained a cycle.
codes.correctiveAction: Send an acyclic JSON-compatible value.

codes.summary: A value was a function, symbol, bigint, or undefined.
codes.correctiveAction: Use only JSON types: null, boolean, finite number, string, array, plain object.

codes.summary: A number was NaN or infinite.
codes.correctiveAction: Use null for a missing observation. A non-finite value is never a measurement.

codes.summary: An object used a prototype-polluting key.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove keys named __proto__, constructor, or prototype.

codes.summary: Reflecting on the value threw. The value is treated as hostile and is not inspected again.
codes.correctiveAction: Pass a plain value. A Proxy whose traps throw cannot be safely snapshotted.

codes.summary: Value nesting exceeded the snapshot depth limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Flatten the value.

codes.summary: The value graph exceeded the total node limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the value or use a data reference.

codes.summary: A string was not well-formed Unicode (it contained a lone surrogate).
codes.correctiveAction: Pass well-formed Unicode strings.

codes.summary: The request did not declare its contract.
codes.correctiveAction: Add {"contract":{"name":"cortexel-figure-request","version":"1.0"}}.

codes.summary: The declared request-contract version is not supported by this build.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a supported version, or run `cortexel migrate`. Compare with `cortexel identity --json`.

codes.summary: The caller supplied a contractDigest that does not match this build's contract digest.
codes.correctiveAction: Omit contractDigest, or pin the exact Cortexel build whose digest you recorded. A mismatch means the contract you validated against is not the contract in use.

codes.summary: The requested skill revision is not supported by this build.
codes.correctiveAction: Omit `skill.revision` to accept the installed revision, or install the build that provides it.

codes.summary: The request failed structural schema validation.
codes.correctiveAction: Correct the value at the reported instancePath. The schemaPath identifies the exact failing keyword.

codes.summary: An object contained a property the contract does not define.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove the property. Stable schemas are closed: a typo in a scientific field must fail rather than be silently ignored.

codes.summary: A required property is absent.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply the property. Cortexel will not infer a scientific fact that the source did not state.

codes.summary: A value had the wrong type.
codes.correctiveAction: Use the declared type. Cortexel performs no type coercion.

codes.summary: A value was outside a closed enumeration.
codes.correctiveAction: Use one of the enumerated values listed in the schema.

codes.summary: The requested skill id is not in the stable catalog.
codes.correctiveAction: Call `cortexel catalog` for the stable ids. A legacy or experimental id is not accepted here.

codes.summary: Parallel arrays that must have equal length do not.
codes.correctiveAction: Ensure the parallel arrays have identical lengths. Cortexel never pairs values with times by best effort.

codes.summary: A declared identifier appeared more than once where identity must be unique.
codes.correctiveAction: Make the ids unique. An ambiguous identity must fail before it can diverge selection, binding, or search.

codes.summary: A row referenced an id that is not in the declared universe.
codes.correctiveAction: Add the id to the declared universe, or remove the row. Cortexel will not silently extend a universe you declared.

codes.summary: A required selection was empty.
codes.correctiveAction: Select at least one member.

codes.summary: A quantity's unit dimension does not match its declared kind, or two combined quantities have incompatible dimensions.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a unit whose dimension matches the quantity kind. Cortexel will not place a concentration and a voltage on one numeric axis because their arrays happen to be the same length.

codes.summary: A stable request used an accepted alias rather than the canonical unit code.
codes.correctiveAction: Use the canonical code from the unit registry. A repair is supplied. Aliases are accepted only by adapters and by `cortexel migrate`, never silently in normal validation.

codes.summary: A conversion was required between units that cannot be converted.
codes.correctiveAction: Simulator-defined units (such as a NEST weight) have no SI mapping and are never converted or pooled.

codes.summary: Histogram bin edges are not strictly increasing, or are not finite.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply strictly increasing finite edges. n edges define n-1 half-open bins.

codes.summary: An analysis window is empty or inverted.
codes.correctiveAction: Require start < stop. The default convention is the half-open interval [start, stop).

codes.summary: Events fall outside the declared observation window.
codes.correctiveAction: Widen the window or exclude the events explicitly. The count of excluded events is always recorded; events are never dropped silently.

codes.summary: A rate denominator is missing, zero, or negative.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply a positive recorded-sender count. Cortexel does NOT infer population size from the number of senders that happened to spike — a silent neuron is still a recorded neuron.

codes.summary: The figure requires a declared recorded-sender universe and none was supplied.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare the senders that were recorded. Without it, a mean-per-neuron rate cannot be computed; use a total-event-rate normalization instead.

codes.summary: The figure requires a declared trial universe or count and none was supplied.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare the trials. Cortexel does not infer trial count from the maximum observed trial id: a trial with no events is still a trial.

codes.summary: An interval was negative after within-train sorting, which indicates invalid input.
codes.correctiveAction: Check the event times. After stable sorting within a train, successive differences cannot be negative.

codes.summary: A zero-length interval was found and the source profile does not permit duplicate same-sender events.
codes.correctiveAction: Either declare that duplicate same-sender events are permitted, or fix the source data. Zero intervals are never silently removed.

codes.summary: A value declared as a count is not a non-negative integer.
codes.correctiveAction: Counts are exact. Supply integers.

codes.summary: A supplied normalized value could not be verified against its raw count and denominator.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply the raw integer count and the denominators, or correct the normalized value. Cortexel re-derives and checks pre-normalized input rather than trusting it.

codes.summary: A density histogram does not integrate to one over its bin widths within tolerance.
codes.correctiveAction: Re-normalize, or use `count` or `probability` normalization instead.

codes.summary: A synaptic delay was zero or negative.
codes.correctiveAction: Delays must be finite and positive. A zero delay is not physical for the supported source simulators.

codes.summary: Weights of different declared dimensions or synapse models were combined.
codes.correctiveAction: Group weights by synapse model or declared dimension. Two numbers are not comparable merely because both are called 'weight'.

codes.summary: Multiple connections map to one cell and no multapse aggregation was declared.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare an aggregation (sum, mean, min, max, or no_aggregation). 'Last edge wins' is never applied.

codes.summary: A series contained duplicate timestamps and no duplicate-time policy was declared.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare reject, keep_replicates, or an explicit aggregate with a method. Last-write-wins is never applied.

codes.summary: The correlogram lag range or bin width is invalid.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply a finite lag range and a positive bin width that tiles it.

codes.summary: A correlation coefficient was requested but its required statistics or a valid variance denominator are absent.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply the required statistics, or use `raw_count` or an explicitly named rate normalization. A scaled pair count is not a correlation coefficient.

codes.summary: Uncertainty bounds are not ordered, not finite, or do not bracket the estimate where the method requires it.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply lower <= estimate <= upper with compatible units and matching keys.

codes.summary: An interval level is outside (0,1), or a required sample count is not positive.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply a valid level and basis. Cortexel never converts an arbitrary range or a standard deviation into a confidence or credible interval.

codes.summary: This figure does not support the supplied uncertainty variant.
codes.correctiveAction: Consult the skill's contract for the supported variants.

codes.summary: A network figure did not declare its scope.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare a NetworkScopeV1. A connection snapshot without scope cannot be interpreted.

codes.summary: A rank-local or sampled snapshot was used to make a global completeness claim.
codes.correctiveAction: Merge every rank's snapshot and declare globalMerged, or keep the output labelled as the partial evidence it is.

codes.summary: An out-degree distribution was requested from a target-rank-local snapshot.
codes.correctiveAction: Under NEST's MPI semantics a rank sees the connections whose TARGET it owns. In-degree is therefore computable for the local target universe; global out-degree is not, until every rank's snapshot has been merged.

codes.summary: A figure whose meaning depends on isolates or zero-degree nodes did not declare its node universe.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare the complete selected node universe. An edge list alone cannot establish that a node has degree zero — it can only establish that no edge was observed.

codes.summary: A merge claimed global coverage but not every rank was present.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply every rank's snapshot. A partial rank set remains partial and cannot be upgraded.

codes.summary: Snapshots being merged disagree on simulation, snapshot time, world size, or connection identity.
codes.correctiveAction: Merge only snapshots from the same run, the same snapshot time, and a consistent world size.

codes.summary: Declared positions do not cover the selected node universe.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply a position for every selected node, or reduce the selection. Nodes with missing positions are reported, never placed at the origin.

codes.summary: The declared scope cannot support this figure's claim.
codes.correctiveAction: Consult the skill contract's legal scope/result combinations.

codes.summary: The request did not declare its source.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare `source.kind`. `unknown` is a valid, honest answer and triggers a disclosure; it is better than invented specificity.

codes.summary: The request tried to set a field that only Cortexel may author.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove it. Validation results, reference-comparison status, accessibility conformance, completeness, output digests, and disclosure text are library-generated facts. A caller declares what its data IS, never what Cortexel concluded about it.

codes.summary: The caller's declared note exceeded its length limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Shorten the note. It is rendered as an attributed, unverified caller statement and cannot displace a mandatory disclosure.

codes.summary: The caller's declared note contained control, bidi-override, or zero-width characters.
codes.correctiveAction: Remove them. Such characters can visually spoof an axis label, a caption, or a mandatory disclosure.

codes.summary: An attestation was supplied whose signature could not be verified.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply a verifiable attestation. A request may never assert that its own attestation is verified.

codes.summary: The request exceeds a hard limit of the active budget profile.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the input. A hard limit protects the process and cannot be raised from untrusted input; it may only be lowered.

codes.summary: A series or the request as a whole exceeds the observation limit.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the data or supply it as a content-addressed DataRef.

codes.summary: A pairwise computation (such as a correlogram) would exceed its cost bound.
codes.correctiveAction: Narrow the lag range, reduce the trains, or select a subset. Cortexel refuses quadratic work rather than attempting it.

codes.summary: The matrix dimensions exceed the cell budget.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the node universes, or reference the data rather than materializing a dense matrix.

codes.summary: The compiled figure would exceed the visible-mark budget and no compaction policy applies.
codes.correctiveAction: Enable a compaction policy that is valid for this figure, or reduce the data. Cortexel never silently truncates.

codes.summary: The serialized output would exceed its byte budget.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the figure's complexity.

codes.summary: The data sidecar would exceed its byte budget.
codes.correctiveAction: Reference the full data by digest instead of embedding it.

codes.summary: The data exceeds the display budget and no scientifically valid compaction exists for this figure.
codes.correctiveAction: Reduce the data. Extrema sampling is invalid for a distribution, and block aggregation is invalid for a matrix without an explicit statistic — so Cortexel refuses rather than misrepresent.

codes.summary: The request referenced data that has not been resolved.
codes.correctiveAction: The host must resolve the reference to verified bytes and pass them in before invoking Cortexel. Stable core performs no network or filesystem access of its own.

codes.summary: Resolved data did not match its declared SHA-256.
codes.correctiveAction: The resolver returned different bytes than the reference declares. This is treated as a failure, never as a warning.

codes.summary: Resolved data did not match its declared byte length.
codes.correctiveAction: Verify byte length and digest before parsing.

codes.summary: The data reference declared an unsupported media type.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a supported interchange profile.

codes.summary: The figure has no valid observations to draw.
codes.correctiveAction: This is reported as an explicit empty state with a reason. Cortexel never draws an empty coordinate system that resembles measured zero.

codes.summary: A scale domain is degenerate and no fallback is defined.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply data with extent, or accept the documented fallback for this figure.

codes.summary: A logarithmic scale was requested for a domain containing zero or negative values.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a linear or symlog scale, or exclude the non-positive values explicitly and accept the disclosure.

codes.summary: A diverging colour scale was requested with no meaningful center.
codes.correctiveAction: Declare the center. A diverging map without a reference point implies a symmetry that the data may not have.

codes.summary: More series were supplied than the visual system can distinguish.
codes.correctiveAction: Use small multiples or group the series. Cortexel refuses to mint dozens of indistinguishable colours.

codes.summary: No stable render-plan compiler exists for this skill.
codes.correctiveAction: This is an internal invariant failure if the skill is stable; report it.

codes.summary: A theme override failed its contrast or format constraints.
codes.correctiveAction: Use an approved semantic token within the documented limits. Raw CSS, URLs, gradients, and font files are never accepted.

codes.summary: A pre-1.0 skill id was used in normal validation.
codes.correctiveAction: Run `cortexel migrate`. Legacy ids are never silently aliased, because a silent alias makes a stored artifact ambiguous about what was actually validated.

codes.summary: The legacy id is not in the migration map.
codes.correctiveAction: Consult MIGRATION.md for every recognized pre-1.0 id.

codes.summary: The legacy capability has no stable 1.0 replacement.
codes.correctiveAction: Consult MIGRATION.md for the alternatives. Animation replay, for example, has no deterministic stable renderer and is experimental.

codes.summary: The legacy `nest.connectivity_matrix` does not say whether it meant adjacency, weight, or delay.
codes.correctiveAction: Choose network.adjacency_matrix, network.weight_matrix, or network.delay_matrix. Cortexel will not guess from the shape of the fields, because the three have different absence, zero, and aggregation semantics.

codes.summary: Migration cannot proceed because the legacy payload lacks information the 1.0 contract requires.
codes.correctiveAction: Supply the missing fact. Migration never guesses a population count, trial count, unit, node universe, MPI completeness, uncertainty, or zero-lag policy.

codes.summary: A legacy value mapped to a weaker but accurate target because the original was ambiguous.
codes.correctiveAction: Review the migration report and state the fact explicitly. An ambiguous source never maps to a stronger status.

codes.summary: The NEST status/recorder object had a structure this adapter version does not support.
codes.correctiveAction: Check the supported NEST version matrix. The observed keys are reported in a bounded, safe summary.

codes.summary: The upstream source version is not in this adapter's certified matrix.
codes.correctiveAction: Use a certified version. Cortexel does not silently widen a support range because a resolver installed something newer.

codes.summary: The adapter needs an explicit mapping the caller did not supply.
codes.correctiveAction: Select the channel, recorded variable, sender identity, and window explicitly. A generic blob with no mapping is rejected.

codes.summary: Adapter input carried accessor properties.
codes.correctiveAction: Pass plain data. The adapter rejects accessor-bearing input before any getter can run.

codes.summary: An experimental capability was requested through a stable entry point.
codes.correctiveAction: Import the explicit experimental subpath. Experimental capabilities carry no stable contract, determinism, or accessibility guarantee.

codes.summary: The capability was removed from the catalog.
codes.correctiveAction: See the migration table for its replacement.

codes.summary: More errors were found than the diagnostic budget permits.
codes.correctiveAction: Fix the reported errors and revalidate. The omitted count is reported so no failure is hidden.

codes.summary: Cortexel detected an internal invariant violation and refused to emit a result.
codes.correctiveAction: This is a Cortexel defect. Please report it with the reproducer. Cortexel fails rather than emit an artifact it cannot vouch for.
